title: Matrix Completion (low-rank recovery from partial entries)
sub_domain: Low-rank matrix recovery via nuclear-norm minimization
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Candes-Recht 2009 exact MC; Recht-Fazel-Parrilo 2010 guaranteed min-rank via nuclear norm; Cai-Candes-Shen 2010 SVT; Keshavan-Montanari-Oh 2010 OptSpace; Jain-Netrapalli-Sanghavi 2013 alt-min.

E.description: A low-rank matrix M* in R^{m x n} of rank r << min(m,n) has only a subset P_Omega(M*) of entries observed. Recover M* from observed entries under low-rank assumption.
E.forward_model: Y = P_Omega(M*) + noise;  P_Omega projects onto observed index set Omega
E.world_state_x: Low-rank matrix M* in R^{m x n}, rank(M*) = r
E.observation_y: Observed entries Y_{ij} for (i,j) in Omega, |Omega| = m_obs
physical_parameters_theta: Coherence mu_0 (concentration of left/right singular vectors)

W.uniqueness: when p >= C * mu_0^2 * r * (m+n) * log^2(m+n) / (m*n) (Candes-Tao 2010)
W.regime: Unique recovery via nuclear-norm minimization under incoherence assumption. Fails when coherence is high (spiky matrices) or observation ratio is below sample-complexity threshold.

C.solver_class: SVT (singular value thresholding), nuclear-norm SDP, alt-min (PowerFactorization), OptSpace, learned (Neural CF, Matrix-Factorization NN)
C.complexity: SVT: O(m*n*r) per iteration; alt-min: O(|Omega| * r) per iteration; SDP: O(m^6) (not scalable)
